bids.reasoning: The opponent opened with their best offer (all valueF, which is worst for me - utility = 0.2 across all issues), giving me a utility of only 0.2. This is very early (t=0.00), so I should counter with my ideal bid to maximize utility. My top utility is achieved with valueA on all issues (utility = 1.0). I'll open strong with my best possible offer.

It's very early (t=0.02), so I can afford to stay near my top utility. The opponent started with all valueF (worst for me) and slightly moved issueA to valueE, showing minimal concession. I should counter with a strong bid but perhaps make a small concession on my least important issue (issueD) to signal willingness to negotiate. My utility for all-A is 1.0; I'll concede issueD to valueB (minor cost: 0.1 * 0.16 = 0.016 loss) to show good faith while staying very high overall.

The opponent seems to be oscillating between their worst offer (all valueF) and only slightly better offers, showing very little concession. We're still very early (t=0.04), so I should stay close to my top utility. My previous bid was issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueA, issueD: valueB (utility ≈ 0.4*1 + 0.3*1 + 0.2*1 + 0.1*0.84 = 0.984). I'll make a tiny concession on issueD (lowest weight) to show willingness to negotiate, while holding firm on the high-weight issues.

The opponent is barely conceding - they've only moved issueA from valueF to valueE and then back to valueF, showing very little flexibility. We're still very early (t=0.06), so I should stay near my top utility while making small concessions. I'll keep issueA and issueB at valueA (highest weight issues), and make a small concession on issueC to valueB while keeping issueD at valueC to show some movement without sacrificing much utility.
